_comment: Diversity / demand factors per BS 7671 Appendix 1 + IEE On-Site Guide Appendix A + NEC Article 220. Applied at panel rollup to convert connected load to maximum demand. Per-discipline percentages, override per-project by editing this file or providing _BIM_COORD/diversity_factors.json.
_standard: BS 7671:2018 App 1 / IEE On-Site Guide / NEC 220

factors.comment: BS 7671 App 1: 100% first 10 A, 50% balance for dwellings; 90% commercial blanket
BS 7671 App 1: first 10 A + 30% balance + 5 A if socket
Thermostat cycling — IEE OSG App A
Diversity for starting kVA + duty cycle
BS 7671 §722 — no diversity unless smart charging
100% per IEE OSG; engineer adjusts for VSD

_neutral_comment: Triplen-harmonic content per load type. Neutral derating per BS 7671 §523.6.3 + IEEE 519. Three-phase neutral current can equal phase current when triplens exceed ~33% — neutral must be sized full or oversized.

harmonics.comment: LED drivers: 15-25% typical; older fluorescent magnetic: 30%+
Mixed IT + small loads via switching PSUs
Switching PSUs without PFC — significant triplens

_pfc_comment: Power-factor correction targets per IEC 61000-3-2 + utility tariff guidance. UK MIC penalties typically apply below PF 0.95.
_spare_comment: Spare-capacity targets per IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B + CIBSE Guide K (typical commercial 25%, industrial 30%, residential 20%, healthcare 35%).
